message:
test(snapshots): add TRACING_SRC_LOC_RE to normalize the tracing source-location segment so dev/release builds produce identical snapshots

Follow-up to the prior snapshot-normalizer work.  The tracing
formatter emits a source-location annotation in dev builds but
omits it in release builds, which manifested as snapshot drift on
release-mode CI:

  dev:     `... DEBUG beads_rust::sync::path: src/sync/path.rs:LINE: Validating sync path …`
  release: `... DEBUG beads_rust::sync::path: Validating sync path …`

Existing `LINE_NUM_RE` already collapsed `.rs:123:` → `.rs:LINE:`,
but the entire `: src/<path>.rs:<line|LINE>:` segment had to be
present-or-absent identically across runs for snapshots to match.
The earlier .snap regenerations dropped this segment by hand, but
that left the normalizer asymmetric: existing recorded snapshots
without the segment matched dev output only after a stale-cache
manual fix.

This change adds `TRACING_SRC_LOC_RE` matching `: src/<...>.rs:<n|LINE>:`
and replaces it with a single `:`, so every output goes through
the same `... beads_rust::module: <message>` shape regardless of
build profile.  The `LINE_NUM_RE` pass below it still handles any
non-tracing `.rs:LINE:` artefacts elsewhere in stdout/stderr.

Logged as `tracing_src_loc` in the normalizer audit trail so test
diagnostics can show that the segment was actually applied.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
